servinfo.title: Rear End Door Assembly Removal and Installation

attention1.ptxt: The rear end door is very heavy with the door balancers detached. The door can cause personal injury if you are not careful when handling it.
Be very careful when opening and closing the rear end door after the door balancers have been detached from the door.

s1.ptxt: Disconnect rear end door harness connectors (1) and washer hose (5).
Remove rear end door balancers (2) from vehicle.
Remove rear end door hinge nuts (4), and then remove rear end door assembly (3) with rear end door hinges.
Remove rear end door hinges, rear end door balancers and/or rear end door opening weather-strip if necessary.

attention1.ptxt: Improperly handling or disposing of the rear end door balancers (1) can result in personal injury.

item.ptxt: Never try to disassemble a balancer. The gas inside the balancer’s cylinder can cause personal injury.

Before discarding any rear end door balancer, a hole must be drilled in the cylinder part using a 2 to 3 mm (0.08 to 0.11 in.) drill (2) to release the inside gas. Be sure to wear goggles when drilling the hole because metal chips produced by the drilling will be propelled by the gas discharged through the hole and may injure your eyes.

attention4.ptxt: Failure to take proper precautions when handling the removed rear end door balancers (dampers) can cause damage to balancers or can cause them not to work properly after reinstallation.

item.ptxt: Do not scratch the exposed surface of the piston rod. Also, do not allow paint or oil to touch the surface of the piston rod.
Do not turn the piston rod with the balancer fully extended.

servinfosub.title: Installation
servinfosub.ptxt: Reverse removal procedure noting the following points.

item.ptxt: Tighten bolts and nuts to specified torque.
Adjust door cushion to prevent door from contacting the body when closed.
Apply sealant to contact face “A” of door hinge (1) and apply grease to rotating part “B” of door hinge (1).
